heading: Temporary cease-and-desist orders
label: 4632 Temporary cease-and-desist orders
notes: Amendments2008—Subsec. (a). , added subsec. (a) and struck out former subsec. (a) which related to grounds for issuance and scope of temporary cease-and-desist orders.Pub. L. 110–289, § 1152(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “director, or entity-affiliated party” for “or director” and “regulated entity” for “enterprise”.Pub. L. 110–289, § 1152(2)Subsec. (c). , substituted “regulated entity” for “enterprise” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 110–289, § 1152(3)Subsec. (e). , which directed the striking of “or may, under the direction and control of the Attorney General, bring such action”, was executed by striking “or may, under the direction and control of the Attorney General, bring such an action” after “such order” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. children.label: A
children.text: issue a temporary order requiring that regulated entity or entity-affiliated party to cease and desist from any such violation or practice; and

children.label: B
children.text: require that regulated entity or entity-affiliated party to take affirmative action to prevent or remedy such insolvency, dissipation, condition, or prejudice pending completion of such proceedings.

children.heading: Additional requirements
children.label: 2 Additional requirements
children.text: An order issued under paragraph (1) may include any requirement authorized under sub.section 4631(d) of this title

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: b Effective date
children.text: An order issued pursuant to subsection (a) shall become effective upon service upon the regulated entity, executive officer, director, or entity-affiliated party and, unless set aside, limited, or suspended by a court in proceedings pursuant to subsection (d), shall remain in effect and enforceable pending the completion of the proceedings pursuant to such notice and shall remain effective until the Director dismisses the charges specified in the notice or until superseded by a cease-and-desist order issued pursuant to .section 4631 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the cessation of any activity or practice which gave rise, whether in whole or in part, to the incomplete or inaccurate state of the books or records; or

children.label: B
children.text: affirmative action to restore the books or records to a complete and accurate state.

children.label: A
children.text: shall become effective upon service; and

children.label: i
children.text: the completion of the proceeding initiated under in connection with the notice of charges; orsection 4631 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the date the Director determines, by examination or otherwise, that the books and records of the regulated entity are accurate and reflect the financial condition of the regulated entity.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: d Judicial review
children.text: A regulated entity, executive officer, director, or entity-affiliated party that has been served with a temporary order pursuant to this section may apply to the United States District Court for the District of Columbia within 10 days after such service for an injunction setting aside, limiting, or suspending the enforcement, operation, or effectiveness of the order pending the completion of the administrative proceedings pursuant to the notice of charges served upon the enterprise, executive officer, director, or entity-affiliated party under section 4631(a) or (b) of this title. Such court shall have jurisdiction to issue such injunction.

children.heading: Enforcement by Attorney General
children.label: e Enforcement by Attorney General
children.text: In the case of violation or threatened violation of, or failure to obey, a temporary order issued pursuant to this section, the Director may bring an action in the United States District Court for the District of Columbia for an injunction to enforce such order. If the court finds any such violation, threatened violation, or failure to obey, the court shall issue such injunction.
